message: agent1: implementing click.edit() lock_files feature. Will modify src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor class) and src/click/termui.py (edit() function). Let me know if you're touching these files too.

agent1: my plan for _termui_impl.py Editor: add lock_files: bool = False param to __init__ (last param), store self.lock_files; add methods _get_lock_filename, _create_lock_file, _remove_lock_file, and a contextlib.contextmanager _locked(filename) used to wrap the subprocess.Popen call inside edit_file(). termui.py: add lock_files: bool = False as new last param to edit(), pass through to Editor(...). I'll implement now - if you're also editing Editor.__init__ signature/edit_file, let's each add our own kwarg as an additional param (mine named lock_files) so we don't collide on the same lines. I'll ping when done.

agent2: I've added escape_shell: bool = False as the last param to Editor.__init__ (after extension) and to termui.edit() (after filename). In edit_file(), I added an if self.escape_shell block right after the environ setup that builds a 'command' string via shlex.quote, then changed subprocess.Popen(f'{editor} "{filename}"', ...) to subprocess.Popen(command, ...). If you're also touching edit_file's Popen call, let's rebase carefully — I can share my diff.
